name: Bread Machine Onion Bagels- Regular Size Batch
recipeInstructions:
1. Presoak 1/4 cup dried onions in 1/3 cup warm water for 10 minutes. Drain and press out excess liquid. Place the specified amount of pre-soaked onions in the machine with the rest of the ingredients. Save some to sprinkle on top of the bagels before baking.
2. Insert ingredients into the bread machine according to manufacturer's instructions. Remove dough from the machine after the first knead, approximately 20-30 minutes.
3. Place dough on a floured surface. Divide into 5 parts. Form balls, gently press thumb through center of each ball and slowly stretch into bagel shape.
4. Boil rapidly boiling water (with 1 tablespoon sugar) in a large saucepan. Drop test dough into the boiling water. Boil each side for 30 seconds, remove and cool on a rack for 1 minute. Brush with egg wash and sprinkle with sesame or poppy seeds if desired.
5. Bake at 400°F on a baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal until golden brown, approximately 15 minutes.
Bagel Success Hints:
When forming the bagels, set aside two small balls of dough. When the bagels have doubled in size, drop them into boiling water. The dough should pop to the top immediately when this happens. A quick spray of non-stick vegetable coating on the top of the bagel may be substituted for the egg wash. To make bagel sticks, cut bagels before rising and lay out in a straight line. Roll sticks in a combination of sesame and poppy seeds with a pinch of garlic powder. Let sticks rise, boil, and bake as described above. To make bagel chips, slice leftover bagels horizontally into thin slices. Brush one side with butter or margarine and place (butter side up) on an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 325°F for 12-15 minutes until golden brown and crisp.
